description: --version returns version JSON
--describe returns full API contract

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL
TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NULLABLE
description: identical schemas → SAFE no-op

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|TABLE posts|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL
description: drop table (posts) → CRITICAL, data loss

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|TABLE posts|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL
description: drop table → CRITICAL, data loss
analyze without --from flag → UNKNOWN_COMMAND (binary treats incomplete analyze as unknown command)
identical schemas → SAFE, no operations (no-op diff baseline)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL
TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email_address text NOT_NULL
description: add nullable column → SAFE/NOTABLE
drop column → CRITICAL, data loss

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL
TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NOT_NULL
description: V0.1.4 regression: NOT_NULL column add must report MEDIUM risk tier
add NOT_NULL column without default → MEDIUM

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|TABLE orders|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL
description: V0.1.4 regression: adding a new table must report SAFE (not NOTABLE)
column type change → CRITICAL (RT-83)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN "DROP TABLE users" serial NOT_NULL
description: set_not_null on existing nullable column → HIGH + decision_required

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email varchar NOT_NULL
description: V39 regression: data-loss migration must include decision_required:true in binary output

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NULLABLE|COLUMN phone text NOT_NULL
description: V0.1.4 regression: mixed nullable+NOT_NULL add must report MEDIUM (highest risk wins)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL
TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email integer NOT_NULL
description: V0.1.4 regression (RT-83): column type change text→integer must report CRITICAL not SAFE

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NULLABLE
TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NOT_NULL
description: V0.1.4 regression (RT-82): SET NOT NULL on existing nullable column must report HIGH + decision_required

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NOT_NULL
TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NULLABLE
description: V0.1.4 regression (RT-82): DROP NOT NULL on existing column must report NOTABLE (safe operation)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL PRIMARY_KEY
description: RT-88: adding PRIMARY_KEY to existing column must report HIGH + decision_required (ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock + full table scan)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL PRIMARY_KEY
description: RT-88: dropping PRIMARY_KEY from existing column must report NOTABLE (brief lock, no data loss)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NULLABLE|TABLE orders|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL PRIMARY_KEY
TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN bio text NULLABLE|TABLE orders|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL PRIMARY_KEY
description: RT-91 regression: identical schemas with PRIMARY_KEY on second table must report SAFE (no cross-pipe scanner contamination)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL UNIQUE
description: RT-92: adding UNIQUE constraint to existing column must report HIGH + decision_required (ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock + full table scan)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL UNIQUE
description: RT-92: dropping UNIQUE constraint from existing column must report NOTABLE (brief lock, no data loss)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL UNIQUE|COLUMN bio text NULLABLE|TABLE orders|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL PRIMARY_KEY
TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL UNIQUE|COLUMN bio text NULLABLE|TABLE orders|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL PRIMARY_KEY
description: RT-99 baseline: multi-table multi-modifier identical schema must report SAFE (no truncation, all modifiers preserved)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN user_id integer NOT_NULL REFERENCES
description: RT-93: adding FOREIGN KEY (REFERENCES) to existing column must report HIGH + decision_required

args: TABLE users|COLUMN user_id integer NOT_NULL REFERENCES
description: RT-93: dropping FOREIGN KEY (REFERENCES) from existing column must report NOTABLE

args: TABLE users|COLUMN score integer NULLABLE DEFAULT
description: RT-87: adding DEFAULT to existing column must report NOTABLE (fast metadata op)

args: TABLE users|COLUMN score integer NULLABLE DEFAULT
description: RT-87: dropping DEFAULT from existing column must report NOTABLE

args: TABLE users|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|TABLE users|COLUMN email text NOT_NULL
description: Test 12: duplicate table name in --from -> INVALID_SCHEMA (prevents false CRITICAL from dedup collision)

args: TABLE gf|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|TABLE hWH|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL
TABLE gf|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|TABLE hWH|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL|TABLE orders|COLUMN id serial NOT_NULL
description: Test 13: dual-hash dedup — djb2 collision pair (gf/hWH) accepted as distinct tables; add_table -> NOTABLE
